title: Unsafe division without zero-check
source_title: DAML Invariant Pattern - Unsafe Decimal Division
description: Division operation where the denominator is a variable not validated against zero. In DAML, division by zero results in a transaction abort, which can be exploited for Denial of Service if the denominator is user-controllable.
recommendation: Add an assertion to ensure the denominator is not zero before performing the division.

oracle.description: Denominator in division must be validated against zero.
oracle.static_signal: Infix operator `/` used with a variable denominator that is not guarded by an assertion.

notes: Numeric precision and division issues are common in financial DAML contracts.
